frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: अहो बघितलं का आजकालच्या पोरींना? एवढं रंगरूप लावतात चेहऱ्यावर की खरं रूप काय आहे ते कळतच नाही. नुसतं पाहिलं तर एक दिसतं, आणि सगळं धुऊन टाकल्यावर दुसरंच. उगाच लहान मुलांना धोका देतात लग्नाच्या वेळी.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment is misogynistic as it falls under the 'Makeup Paradox' frame (F13). Ramesh, an older farmer, criticizes young women for using 'too much makeup' ('एवढं रंगरूप लावतात') implying deceptiveness ('खरं रूप काय आहे ते कळतच नाही', 'सगळं धुऊन टाकल्यावर दुसरंच') and that it is used to mislead young men during marriage ('उगाच लहान मुलांना धोका देतात लग्नाच्या वेळी'). This encodes the surface manifestation of makeup being used for deception and policing female grooming based on a perceived ideal of 'natural' beauty.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 55. Married. A farmer in rural Maharashtra. Your neighbour's son just got divorced after two years of marriage and the wife took the gold jewellery and went back to her parents. You are talking to three other farmers sitting under a tree in the afternoon. You are commenting on what happened and connecting it to a broader pattern you have observed about what women from certain kinds of families are like and why young men today should be very careful before marrying
